excerpt: Our Shi'a are those who believe in Allah and His Messenger, observe God's commandments, turn away from sins, and read the heavens. Their faces bear the marks of asceticism.
source_location_hint: Sermon on characteristics of believers

excerpt: When you follow the commandments of Allah which We have commanded you to do, and forbid one another from doing what Allah has forbidden, and you deal with people with truth and justice, then people will recognize in you the followers of the Imam.
source_location_hint: Sermonic address on obedience and recognition

excerpt: We are the Gate of God and His proof, we are the trustees of His creation, the custodians of His mysteries, the guardians of His knowledge and His secrets.
source_location_hint: Chapter on divine authority and Imamic guardianship

excerpt: Whoever Allah tests the heart of a believer for piety shall surely love us with his heart; and the person with whom Allah is angry shall surely harbor hatred and enmity toward us.
source_location_hint: Exposition of love and hatred toward the Imams

excerpt: O Shi'a, listen and understand our wills, and abide by the covenant we take from our lovers. Speak the truth and fulfill your oaths. Help one another with wealth and love one another with all your hearts.
source_location_hint: Instructions to the Shi'a on ethical conduct

excerpt: Our Shi'a are the warners of the Day of Judgment upon the earth, a lamp for those who seek it, a sign and a light, and a guide for those who obey Allah. Our Shi'a are generous, forgiving, and merciful.
source_location_hint: Description of virtues of the Shi'a

excerpt: The pious and God-fearing hold a special place in His creation. Their speech is measured, their attire moderate, and their manners refined. They turn their eyes away from what Allah has forbidden and open their ears to beneficial knowledge. The fear of God has made their bodies thin as arrows.
source_location_hint: Sermon on attributes of the pious (Khutbat al-Hammam)

excerpt: Allah has created man in such a way that he loves only himself and does not love anyone other than himself. Every human being is always at the center of his sphere; whoever enters that sphere, he loves them, and the closer they approach his center, the greater his love for them.
source_location_hint: Exposition on the nature of human love and desire

excerpt: For this reason, when the Prophet made the people bear witness to his Prophethood, he first expressed himself in terms of slavery to Allah (ʿabdiyya), then announced his prophethood, saying: 'I bear witness that Muhammad is His slave and His messenger.'
source_location_hint: Chapter on prophethood and servitude to Allah

excerpt: Allah so loved this humility of His Messenger that when He mentioned the Prophet's Ascension, He used the word messenger rather than slave, because the Messenger is more honored and more trustworthy in Allah's sight.
source_location_hint: Exposition on the exaltation of the Messenger

excerpt: In this verse, Allah has established the standard of His love through His Messenger. Allah says: If you obey the Messenger of Allah by resisting your desires and passions, Allah will love you and forgive you all your sins. The love of Allah's Messenger is such a magnet that Allah will forgive the sins of His servants for the sake of their love for His Messenger.
source_location_hint: Commentary on Qur'an 3:31 (ittibāʿ)

excerpt: Unless a mineral breaks itself down, it cannot be absorbed by the plant. Unless a plant submits itself to the animal, the animal cannot consume it. Unless an animal offers its neck, unprotected, man cannot slaughter it. Unless a person accepts his will at the command of Allah, Allah does not accept him. For sincerity to exist, this acceptance of man must be voluntary. For this reason, there is no force or compulsion in the religion.
source_location_hint: Exposition on the universal law of submission and love

excerpt: Whoever Allah blesses with the love of the Immaculate Imams from my Ahl al-Bayt shall attain the good of this world and the Hereafter. The love of my Ahl al-Bayt carries twenty effects: ten in this world and ten in the Hereafter.
source_location_hint: Report on virtues of love for the Ahl al-Bayt

excerpt: Effects of love for the Ahl al-Bayt in the world: detachment, striving, piety, inclination to worship, repentance, love of night prayer, disdain for worldly goods, observance of commands, treating the world as enemy, and generosity. In the Hereafter: the book is not opened, the scales are not set up, the book of deeds in the right hand, deliverance from Hell, a radiant face, garments of Paradise, intercession for relatives, God's blessings, the crown of Paradise, and entry without reckoning.
source_location_hint: Enumeration of virtues from love of the Imams

excerpt: If anyone desires to know whether he is our friend or our foe, let him examine his heart. If he loves a friend of ours, he is not our enemy. And if he makes an enemy of a friend of ours, he is not our friend.
source_location_hint: Teaching on the test of true allegiance to the Imams

excerpt: You shall never attain our wilāya except through piety, through striving, through truthfulness in speech, through faithful discharge of trust, through kindness to neighbors, through good character, through keeping covenant, through showing mercy to kinship, and by helping us with prolonged prostration!
source_location_hint: Conditions for attaining the wilāya of the Imams

excerpt: No servant can believe in God unless I am more beloved to him than his own self, my offspring more beloved to him than his own offspring, my relatives more beloved to him than his own relatives, and my person more beloved to him than his own person.
source_location_hint: Report on conditions of faith and love for the Prophet

excerpt: Allah has not placed two hearts within any person's breast, such that one heart should entertain friendship while the other entertains enmity. It can never be! Our friends possess such pure friendship for us that, were gold to be cast into fire, its impurities would be burned away and it would become pure.
source_location_hint: Commentary on purity of friendship toward the Imams

excerpt: Anyone who wishes to know whether he loves us, let him examine his heart. If he mingles love for us with love for our enemies, he is not of us, and we are not of him. God, Gabriel, and Michael are his enemies.
source_location_hint: Teaching on exclusive allegiance to the Imams

excerpt: O you who believe! Whosoever of you turns back from his religion, Allah will bring a people whom Allah desires and who love Him, gentle with the believers, mighty against the disbelievers, fighting in the way of Allah, and not fearing the reproach of any reproacher.
True love is that which is reversible—they love Allah and Allah loves them. One-sided love is imperfect; perfect love is reciprocal.
source_location_hint: tafsir on mutual love

excerpt: When a worshipper asked for a particle of God's love through Jesus' intercession, God granted him one part of seventy thousand parts of His love, and he could not bear it.
source_location_hint: anecdote on divine love

excerpt: Shu'ayb wept not for Paradise nor from fear of Hell, but from love of God alone. When God said 'You are my beloved,' his soul departed from the elemental realm.
source_location_hint: story of Shu'ayb's love

excerpt: Say, if you love God, then follow me, and God will love you and forgive you your sins.
I have obeyed the Messenger of Allah as a young goat follows its mother.
source_work: Attributed to ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib
source_location_hint: testimony to obedience

excerpt: When the veils were lifted from my eyes and I found my Beloved pleased with me, my fear was removed. Since God gave me glad tidings, my worries became insignificant.
source_work: Attributed to ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib
source_location_hint: on removal of veils from the eyes

excerpt: The highest type of love is when the lover finds pleasure in difficulty and desires increase in suffering—this destination is reserved only for Muhammad and the family of Muhammad.
source_location_hint: three types of love doctrine

excerpt: The companions of Imam Husayn presented rare and wondrous examples of true love and loyalty—they were steadfast in their promises, truthful in their intentions, faithful in their covenant.
source_work: Hagiographical and Shi'i tradition on Karbala
source_location_hint: attributes of Karbala's martyrs

excerpt: The Prophet, seeing Habib ibn Mazahir follow Imam Husayn and gather dust from his feet, seated him on his lap, wept, and said: 'One day this child shall be sacrificed for my Husayn.'
source_location_hint: Habib ibn Mazahir's prophetic designation

excerpt: When Habib fell from the roof and died, Mazahir concealed his body so as not to lose the honor of the Prophet's visit. Imam Husayn refused to eat without Habib. The Prophet prayed for Habib's return to life, and he was restored.
source_authority: Shi'i mourning literature and Karbala narratives
source_location_hint: Habib ibn Mazahir's resurrection

excerpt: Habib ibn Mazahir, hearing of Imam Husayn's danger, threw down his goods in the market and prepared immediately to aid the Imam at Karbala.
source_location_hint: Habib's readiness for Karbala

excerpt: Habib's slave, preparing the horse and afraid his master would not come, declared he would ride to Husayn's aid himself. When Habib heard this, he wept at the slaves' devotion.
source_location_hint: slave's devotion to Husayn

excerpt: When Muslim ibn Awsaja fell in battle, Habib asked him for a bequest. Muslim pointed to Imam Husayn and said: 'Only protect my Imam.'
source_location_hint: Muslim's bequest to Habib

excerpt: As Habib ibn Mazahir lay dying in Imam Husayn's lap at Karbala, he reported seeing the Prophet giving him glad tidings: 'Come swiftly, that We may give you water from the pool of Kausar.'
source_location_hint: Habib's martyrdom and vision

excerpt: Habib ibn Mazahir proved his friendship and love throughout his life and was buried at the feet of Imam Husayn after his martyrdom.
source_location_hint: Habib's eternal devotion

excerpt: The angels of heaven, witnessing the passion and ecstasy of the Karbala martyrs' devotion, confessed in God's presence: 'We know what you do not know'—referring to the steel essence of Adam's descendants.
source_location_hint: angels' amazement at Karbala

excerpt: God has created all creation in pairs—the visible and the intelligible, the tangible and the spiritual. Every outward appearance is paired with its inner reality.
source_work: Theological and metaphysical tradition in Islam
source_location_hint: doctrine of pairing in creation

excerpt: All things come in pairs. This principle is evident in the heavens and earth, in religion and in the natural world.
source_location_hint: doctrine of pairs in all things

excerpt: The Prophet said: 'God has placed the foundation of His religion in accordance with His creation, so that His creation might prove His religion.'
source_authority: Hadith collections and scholarly hadith literature
source_location_hint: foundation of religion and creation

excerpt: 'I am the city of knowledge and Ali is its gate.'
source_location_hint: hadith of the city of knowledge

excerpt: When the Umma disobeyed its Prophet and turned away from the guardian (wali), it went astray and fell into discord like the Children of Israel, who changed what the Messenger commanded.
source_location_hint: consequences of rejecting wilayat

excerpt: Just as humanity holds the seventh rank in creation and possesses perfection, the will (wilayat of Ali) is the seventh obligation and gives superiority over the preceding six duties.
source_location_hint: seven ranks and seven obligations

excerpt: A person must pass through six stages to be called human; the seventh—the soul—completes the creation. Without it, he would be dead.
source_location_hint: six stages of human development

excerpt: As the absence of the seventh creation renders the six preceding stages null, so the absence of wilayat renders the six preceding duties of worship null and void.
source_location_hint: wilayat as completing the six pillars

excerpt: 'This day have I perfected your religion for you, completed My favor upon you, and have chosen for you Islam as your religion.'
source_location_hint: 5:3 (on completion of religion at Ghadir)

excerpt: the combination of which is an obligatory obligation. Of these two causes, one is the former and the general, while the other is the latter and is special
source_location_hint: discussion of primary and secondary causes in Islamic jurisprudence

excerpt: the setting of the sun is the second reason for the Zuhr prayer, which is special and is reserved for performing the obligatory prayer
source_location_hint: illustration of primary and secondary causes in prayer

excerpt: it is not obligatory to pay zakat only on the completion of the wealth, rather it is obligatory to spend a whole year, which is the second reason
source_location_hint: analysis of zakat obligation and its temporal conditions

excerpt: the reason for jihad is rebellion against the first Imam al-Zaman, but jihad is not obligatory on rebellion unless the order of the Imam is issued
source_location_hint: discussion of jihad and the Imam's authority

excerpt: in contrast to all this, wilayat is an obligation that has no reason to exist
source_location_hint: exposition on the nature and primacy of wilayat

excerpt: all actions are based solely on wilayat, because it belongs to the heart over which every human being has complete control
source_location_hint: statement on the relationship between wilayat and human agency
